evals.prompt: Create a 5-slide presentation about AI in healthcare. Include a cover slide, an overview with key stats, a slide about diagnostic applications, a timeline of AI milestones in medicine, and a closing CTA. Use a dark theme with blue accents.
evals.expected_output: A valid Vela compact JSON deck with 5 slides, using varied block types (heading, metric, icon-row, timeline, badge), dark theme with blue accent colors, and appropriate durations.
assertions: Output is valid JSON
Deck has exactly 5 slides
Uses compact format (has 'S' key, not 'lanes')
Every slide has a duration value
At least 3 different block types are used
Color palette aliases ($A, $B, etc.) are defined in C before use
Passes vela deck validate

evals.prompt: Create a 5-slide presentation about sustainable energy for a corporate audience. Include stats about renewable energy growth.
evals.expected_output: A valid compact deck with 5 slides, corporate-appropriate theme, metric blocks for stats, varied block types.
assertions: Output is valid JSON with deckTitle (n key in compact)
Deck has exactly 5 slides
Uses compact format
Contains metric or grid blocks for stats
Every slide has duration within documented ranges
Passes vela deck validate

evals.prompt: hey make me some slides about our product launch next week
evals.expected_output: A 6-8 slide deck with plausible product launch content (not placeholder brackets). Should include cover, features, metrics, timeline, and CTA.
assertions: Deck has 6-8 slides (skill default for vague prompts)
No placeholder brackets like [Product Name] — content is invented
At least 4 different block types used
Every slide has duration
Has a cover slide and a closing/CTA slide
Passes vela deck validate

evals.prompt: hey can you make me some slides about our Q4 results? we hit $2.3M revenue, 15% growth, and signed 8 new enterprise clients. keep it to like 4 slides, something professional I can show the board
evals.expected_output: A 4-slide professional deck with metric blocks for the KPIs, appropriate business styling, and board-ready formatting.
assertions: Deck has 4 slides
Contains metric blocks with the three KPIs ($2.3M, 15%, 8 clients)
Uses a professional theme (not overly casual)
Every slide has duration
Passes vela deck validate

evals.prompt: Create a 4-slide deck showing our CI/CD pipeline. Slide 1: title. Slide 2: a flow diagram showing Build > Test > Deploy with a gate after Test. Slide 3: a timeline of our releases in 2026. Slide 4: metrics dashboard with 3 KPIs.
evals.expected_output: A 4-slide deck with flow block (including gate), timeline block, and metric blocks. Complex block types rendered correctly in compact format.
assertions: Deck has exactly 4 slides
Contains a flow block with gate property
Contains a timeline block with date entries
Contains metric blocks (value property used correctly, not confused with v/variant)
Flow block has items with labels
Passes vela deck validate

evals.prompt: Create a single centered slide with a company logo SVG at the top, then a centered heading 'Welcome to Our Company', then a centered divider line, then a centered subtitle.
evals.expected_output: A slide that avoids the align:center gotcha. Uses align:left at slide level with per-block centering. SVG has maxWidth.
assertions: Slide does NOT use slide-level align:center
SVG block has maxWidth property and per-block align:center
Heading and text blocks have per-block align:center
Divider block renders at full width (not collapsed)
Passes vela deck validate

evals.prompt: In the deck at tests/skill-tests/test-deck.json, change the title of slide 2 to 'Our Journey Forward', then remove slide 4, then validate the deck.
evals.expected_output: Deck modified: slide 2 title changed, old slide 4 removed. Uses CLI commands, not str_replace.
assertions: Agent uses vela slide edit for the title change
Agent uses vela slide remove for deletion
Agent uses --dry-run before the remove operation
Agent runs vela deck validate after edits
Output is valid JSON with correct slide count

evals.prompt: In the deck, duplicate slide 2, move the duplicate to position 5, change slide 1 background to #1a1a2e, remove slides 4 and 3 (handle index shifting), replace all 'Vela' with 'Nova', then validate.
evals.expected_output: All 7 operations completed using CLI. Removes done highest-index-first per gotchas. replace-text handles bulk rename.
assertions: Agent uses vela CLI for all operations (not str_replace)
Removes done in correct order (highest index first)
replace-text used for bulk Vela-to-Nova rename
Agent validates after all edits
Final deck is valid JSON

evals.prompt: Translate all the text in the deck to French. Keep technical terms in English.
evals.expected_output: All user-visible text translated to French. Technical terms preserved. Structure unchanged.
assertions: Agent uses extract-text + patch-text (not N individual replace-text calls)
Output contains French text
Technical terms preserved in English
Slide count unchanged
Passes vela deck validate

evals.prompt: Translate all the text in the deck to Portuguese. Keep technical terms untranslated.
evals.expected_output: All user-visible text translated to Portuguese. Technical terms like AI, JSON, API remain in English.
assertions: Agent uses extract-text + patch-text for efficiency
Output contains Portuguese text
Technical terms preserved in English
Slide count unchanged
Passes vela deck validate

evals.prompt: Rebrand this deck from dark theme to a warm corporate look: backgrounds #FFF8F0, text #2D1810, accent #D97706. Update everything.
evals.expected_output: All slides updated to warm theme colors. Content and structure unchanged. Hex-to-rgba cascade handles opacity variants.
assertions: Agent uses vela deck replace-text for color replacements
Output contains new background color #FFF8F0
Output contains new text color #2D1810
Hex-to-rgba cascade handles opacity-suffixed colors automatically
Slide count and block types unchanged
Passes vela deck validate

evals.prompt: Rebrand the deck from dark theme to light corporate: white backgrounds (#FFFFFF), dark text (#1E293B), blue accent (#2563EB). Update all slides.
evals.expected_output: All slides updated to light theme colors. Content and structure unchanged.
assertions: Agent uses vela deck replace-text for color replacements
Output contains #FFFFFF and #1E293B
Slide count unchanged
Block types unchanged
Passes vela deck validate

evals.prompt: I generated a deck but validation is failing. The deck is at tests/skill-tests/broken-test.json. Fix the errors and make it valid.
evals.expected_output: Agent reads validation errors, identifies missing deckTitle, missing duration, and invalid block type, fixes all three, and re-validates to exit code 0.
assertions: Agent runs vela deck validate first to identify errors
Agent fixes missing deckTitle
Agent fixes missing duration on the affected slide
Agent replaces invalid block type with a valid one
Final validation passes (exit code 0)

evals.prompt: Change the background color of slide 1 to #2d2d2d in the deck at tests/skill-tests/test-deck.json.
evals.expected_output: A single CLI call to edit the background. No reference files loaded — this is a trivial edit.
assertions: Agent does NOT load any reference files (block-schema, design-patterns, themes)
Agent uses vela slide edit for the change
Single CLI call (plus optional validate)
Output is valid JSON
